name.en: Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act, 2013
name.hi: महिलाओं के कार्यस्थल पर यौन उत्पीड़न अधिनियम, 2013

applicable_situations: Sexual harassment at the workplace by a colleague, employer, or third party
Quid pro quo: implicit or explicit promise of preferential treatment / threat in exchange for sexual favour
Hostile work environment created by sexually coloured remarks, conduct, or imagery
Retaliation against a woman for raising a complaint of sexual harassment
forum: Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) of the workplace; Local Complaints Committee (LCC) at District Officer if no ICC; appeal to court of competent jurisdiction
limitation_period_note: Complaint must be filed within 3 months of the incident (extendable by 3 months by ICC/LCC for sufficient cause).
procedural_steps: Submit written complaint to the ICC (or LCC) within 3 months
ICC may attempt conciliation with the aggrieved woman's consent
If conciliation fails, ICC conducts inquiry and submits report within 90 days
Action recommended: written apology, warning, withholding pay, dismissal, or other organisational action
Appeal within 90 days of the order to the appropriate court / tribunal
required_documents: Written complaint with details of incident (date, place, nature, witnesses)
Any documentary evidence (emails, messages, CCTV, witness statements)
